sections.paragraph:
to spread out, to expand (Syr. ܫܛܰܚ, Arab. سَطَحَ, Ethiop. ሰጥሐ፡ id.). Job 12:23, לַגֹּויִם שֹׁטֵחַ “he spreads out the peoples,” i.e. gives them ample territories; especially, to spread out something on the ground, e.g. bones, Jer 8:2 quails, Num 11:32 polenta, 2Sa 17:19.

Piel, to spread out (one’s arms), Psa 88:10.

Derivatives, מִשְׁטָח, מִשְׁטֹוחַ.
